feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Is there a BTP service or API to track user login/logout times and session duration?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a project in SAP BTP and would like to know if there is any built-in service or API that can provide information such as:

  • When a user logged in

  • When they logged out

  • How long they stayed logged in (session duration)

This would help us monitor usage patterns and possibly audit access times.

Is there a service (like Audit Log, Identity Authentication, or another BTP capability) that offers this kind of data? If so, how can it be accessed?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Fiori Elements Action: Navigation Not Triggering After Returning New Instance
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working with SAP Fiori Elements (V4) and CAP, and I have an action defined using @ui.DataFieldForAction that creates a new record with updated effective dates. The backend correctly returns a new instance (confirmed via HTTP 200 OK with full entity data), and I've set navigateToInstance: true in the manifest.json.

Despite this, the navigation to the object page of the newly created instance is not happening.

Here’s what I’ve already checked:

  • The action returns a single entity with a new ID.
  • Object page target is defined for target entity.
  • navigateToInstance: true is set under actions.
  • Backend response includes all key fields and metadata context.
// <service.js> - when action triggers, this below is executed
        const { ID } = req.params[0];
        const { targetentity } = cds.entities('MyService')
        if (ID) {
            const record = await SELECT.one.from(targetentity).where({ ID });
            const newID = uuid();
            console.log("New ID", newID);

            if (record) {
                // Logic to change the effective date
                record.effective_start_date = req.data.eStartDate;
                record.effective_end_date = req.data.eEndDate;
                record.ID = newID;
                await INSERT.into(targetentity).entries(record);

                // ✅ Return the new instance explicitly
                const newRecord = await SELECT.one.from(IARuleForDerivations).where({ ID: newID });
                req.reply(newRecord);
/* I try these things also
*  return newRecord
*/ 
            } else {
                throw new Error("Record not found.");
            }
        } else {
            throw new Error("ID is undefined.");
        }
// <service.cds> Entity Projection with actions
@odata.draft.enabled
entity targetentity as projection on db.targetentity
   actions {
    action changeEffectiveDateToNewRecord(
@UI.ParameterDefaultValue: in.effective_start_date
eStartDate : db.targetentity: effective_start_date,

@UI.ParameterDefaultValue: in.effective_end_date
eEndDate : db.targetentity: effective_end_date) 
returns targetentity;
                };

  Is there anything else I need to configure in the manifest.json or annotations to ensure navigation is triggered correctly?

Thanks in advance,
Kirubakaran K

entry.title: How do I return an entity if it already exists?
entry.content:
I have a simple entity in CAP:

entity Links : cuid, managed {
  url : String @mandatory;
}

I already forbidden access to a bulk read of all the Links in the service, since I don't want users to do that.

However, when a user POSTs a creation of a new Link, if it already exists, I want to ignore the creation of it and return the already existing one while also updating the "modifiedAt/By" of the entity.

I'm achieving this right now by hooking the following event in my service:

this.on("CREATE", Links, (req, next) => { 
  const { Links } = this.entities;
  const { url } = req.data;
  const entity = await SELECT.one.from(Links).where({ url });
  if (entity) {
    await UPDATE.entity(Links).with().where({ ID: entity.ID });
    return req.reply(entity);
  }
  return next();
});

The update is there just to trigger the modifiedAt/By.

My question is: is this the right approach? There are some issues with this current approach, such as returning more fields when the entity already exists making it clear it was a repetition. Any help with finding a better approach is welcome!

entry.title: How to turn off network access logs but keep cds.log in logging on CAP?
entry.content: I noticed by default CAP logs the network access ( the logs with prefix[cds] ) as part of logging message, but we already log these from network level, how to turn it off? I searched the CAP doc no luck. I hope it only logs what we set in cds.log("...") info level - excluding the network logs.

entry.title: Handle "MaxListenersExceededWarning" which calling multiple external API's in SAP CAPM
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are doing a quarterly cleanup of shadow users from BTP cockpit based on the last logon date. We are using the XSUAA API to fetch the users from a Subaccount. As we have multiple subaccounts I have called the Central Information Service to get the list of Subaccounts and then for all subaccounts I fetch the users from each subaccount. Sequence is:

  1. Fetch all subaccounts using cds.connect.to('subaccountdest')
  2. Fetch users in subaccounts via promise all for parallel processing calling each subaccount via cds.connect.to('dynamincssubacdest')

I have the below code:

async function getXsuaaConnection(subdomain) {
    const dest = `xsuaa-api-${subdomain}`;
    try {
        if (!xsuaaConnections[dest]) {
            xsuaaConnections[dest] = await cds.connect.to(dest);
        }
        return xsuaaConnections[dest];
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(`❌ Failed to connect to destination ${dest}:`, err.message);
        throw new Error(`Connection to ${dest} failed`);
    }
}

async function getUsersNew(xsuaa, saDomain, saID, saName) {
    try {
        const xsuaaResp = await xsuaa.get('/Users?count=500');
        const xsuaaUsers = xsuaaResp.resources || [];
        return {
            users: xsuaaUsers.map(user => ({
                userName: user.userName,
                externalId: user.externalId,
                btpId: user.id,
                firstName: user.name?.givenName,
                lastName: user.name?.familyName,
                displayName: `${user.name?.givenName || ''} ${user.name?.familyName || ''}`,
                eMail: user.emails?.[0]?.value || null,
                origin: user.origin,
                isActive: user.active,
                isVerified: user.verified,
                lastLogon: getDate(user.lastLogonTime),
                parent_ID: saID,
                subName: saName,
                subDomain: saDomain,
                createdOn: user.meta?.created
            }))
        };
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(`❌ Failed to fetch users for subdomain ${saDomain}:`, err.message);
        return { users: [] };
    }
}

async function getSa() {
    try {
        const subAccResp = await cis.get('/accounts/v1/subaccounts');
        let subAccs = Array.isArray(subAccResp?.value) ? subAccResp.value : [];

        subAccs = subAccs.filter(sa =>
            sa.subdomain !== 'appdev' && sa.subdomain !== 'ext-portal'
        );

        const subAccounts = subAccs.map(sa => ({
            id: sa.guid,
            name: sa.displayName,
            region: sa.region,
            subDomain: sa.subdomain,
            desc: sa.description
        }));

        const userResults = await Promise.all(
            subAccs.map(async sa => {
                try {
                    const xsuaa = await getXsuaaConnection(sa.subdomain);
                    return await getUsersNew(xsuaa, sa.subdomain, sa.guid, sa.displayName);
                } catch (err) {
                    console.error(`⚠️ Skipping subaccount ${sa.subdomain}:`, err.message);
                    return { users: [] };
                }
            })
        );

        const users = userResults.flatMap(r => r.users);
        return { subaccounts: subAccounts, users };
    } catch (err) {
        console.error('❌ Failed to fetch subaccounts:', err.message);
        return { subaccounts: [], users: [] };
    }
}

As we have more than 10 calls I get the below warning(In future we can have more calls as well) :

"MaxListenersExceededWarning: Possible EventEmitter memory leak detected. 11 succeeded listeners added to [EventEmitter]"

Presently I have increased the limit of Event Emitters and warning is gone, but I have read that this is not a best practice. 

Any help will be appreciated.

@qmacro 

Regards

Braham

entry.title: How to Upload .mtaext File to a Node in SAP Cloud Transport Management via API?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm working with SAP Cloud Transport Management (TMS) and looking to upload .mtaext files to specific nodes (e.g., QA, PROD) after the corresponding .mtar file has been uploaded and transported.

While exploring the Project "Piper" source code, I came across a method UploadMtaExtDescriptorToNode, which sends a POST request to the following endpoint:

/v2/nodes/{nodeId}/mtaExtDescriptors

It posts a .mtaext file via multipart/form-data, along with mtaVersion and description fields. I tried using this endpoint directly via Postman, but it resulted in an exception (500 Internal Server Error). I also noticed that this endpoint is not documented in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, which makes it unclear whether this API is supported or deprecated.

My question:

Is there any recommended way to upload .mtaext files via API after .mtar has been uploaded and transported?

Any guidance would be appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: invalid lob locator id (piecewise lob reading) Error after upgrading to CAP 9.0.3 and Node.js 20
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am encountering a critical error in my CAP Node.js application after upgrading my dependencies. I would appreciate any help or insights you can provide.

Problem Description:

My Node.js service, which uses @sap/cds, fails with the following error when trying to read an Excel file from a HANA database BLOB column:

error while parsing protocol: invalid lob locator id (piecewise lob reading)

The error occurs specifically in the readExcelSheets function, which reads a Readable stream and converts it into a Buffer. This code worked perfectly before the upgrade.

My Environment & Dependencies:

I recently updated my project to use Node.js 20 and the latest major version of @sap/cds. Here are my relevant dependencies from package.json:

"dependencies": {
  "@cap-js/hana": "^2.1.1",
  "@sap/cds": "^9.0.3",
  "xlsx": "^0.18.5"
},
"devDependencies": {
  "@sap/cds-dk": "^9.0.5",
  "@types/node": "^22.13.1"
}

My SAP HANA Database Version:

My SAP HANA database is running on the following version, as shown in the screenshot from my landscape: 2025.14.5.

Relevant Code Snippet:

The error originates from the following code block in my readExcelSheets function:

 
const readExcelSheets = async (content: Readable | Buffer): Promise<Record<string, any[]>> => {
  let buffer: Buffer;
  if (content instanceof Readable) {
    const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
    for await (const chunk of content) { // <-- The error occurs here
      chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
    }
    buffer = Buffer.concat(chunks);
  } else {
    buffer = Buffer.isBuffer(content) ? content : Buffer.from(content);
  }

  const workbook = XLSX.read(buffer, { type: "buffer" });
  const result: Record<string, any[]> = {};

  // ... rest of the function
  return result;
}

The content variable is fetched from the database using a standard CDS SELECT query:

 
const pendings = await db.run(
  SELECT.from("ExcelUploads")
    .columns("ID", "content", "createdAt")
    .where({ progress: "Pending" })
);

Steps I have taken to troubleshoot:

  1. I initially thought the issue was related to the @sap/cds upgrade.

  2. Based on my research, I suspected a HANA client driver incompatibility with Node.js 20.

  3. I ran npm install @sap/hana-client to update the HANA client driver.

  4. After installing the latest @sap/hana-client (which is likely pulled by @cap-js/hana), I received a new, more specific error:

    [cds] - ❗️Uncaught Error: Invalid Object

    The stack trace points directly to the @sap/hana-client module:

    at Socket.<anonymous> (C:\...\node_modules\@sap\hana-client\lib\index.js:53:13)
at Socket.emit (node:events:519:28)
...
at Readable.push (node:internal/streams/readable:390:5)
at Pipe.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:191:23)
at Pipe.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:130:17) {
  code: -20001,
  sqlState: 'HY000',
  query: false
}
  • Has anyone else experienced this invalid lob locator id or Invalid Object error with @sap/cds 9.x and Node.js 20?

  • Given my HANA version 2025.14.5, is there a specific, compatible version of @sap/hana-client that I should be using with Node.js 20?

  • Is there a CDS configuration setting to alter how BLOB data is read (e.g., to read the entire object at once instead of in pieces)?

Thank you for your time and help!

entry.title: Upgrading to CAP CDS 8: When using the $batch request, req.req.authInfo is undefined.
entry.content:
In our CDS 7 version project, getting a user's email is written like this. We can normally get the user's information.

req.req.authInfo.getEmail();

But in the CDS 8 version, using the same method, you can only get it normally if the request is not a $batch request.

Looking at the source code, we found that the value of cds.env.features.odata_new_adapter in version cds8 is set to true by default, and the new adapter is being used. Is it possible that the new adapter didn't process the authInfo for the $batch request?

The authentication method we use is xsuaa. So in the cds8 version, should we use cds.context.attr or req.user.attr to obtain user information?

entry.title: what is the odatav2 api to add a material assignment to production routing header
entry.content:
Hello experts,

i've been using the API_PRODUCTION_ROUTING api to post a new material assignment to SAP public cloud.

somehting like this:

query = `/ProductionRoutingHeader(ProductionRouting='${ProductionRout}', ProductionRoutingGroup='${ProductionRoutGroup}',ProductionRoutingInternalVers='${ProductionRoutiVers}')/to_MatlAssgmt`
and then post the product and plant in the payload.
 
but it seems this got deprecated. Is there a replacement for this? i cant seem to find the same type of endpoint.
Thank you.

entry.title: SAP CAP ValueList Displaying a deep value list
entry.content:
This is my current code for the entity Trainee.

entity Trainee {
    key employee : Association to one Employee;
    currentlyLearning : Association to one lpn.LearningPlanEnrollment;
}

And this is the current code for the entity Employee (just so you guys can understand what I'm trying to do)

entity Employee : cuid {
    firstName    : String(100) @mandatory;
    lastName     : String(100);
    name         : String = firstName || ' ' || lastName;
    gender       : Gender @mandatory;
    email        : String(255) @mandatory;
    dateOfBirth  : Date @mandatory;
    batch        : Integer @mandatory;
}

What I'm currently trying to do is to create a value list inside a custom action where it displays all of the information of the employee from the Trainee entity. In other words, I want the value list to not only display the ID, but also the name, gender, email, dateOfBirth, and batch.

But using the annotation, I can't do it, as if the CDS couldn't read it. Instead, I can only reach this far.

... 
   },
    ) actions { 
        enrollTrainees(
            @Common.ValueList : {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
                CollectionPath : 'Trainees',
                Parameters : [
                    {
                        $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                        LocalDataProperty : trainees,
                        ValueListProperty : 'employee_ID',
                    },
                ],
            }
            trainees
        );
    };
...

 Does anyone have a solution to what I'm trying to do? If using Fiori Elements not possible, is it possible using Fiori Freestyle? Thank you.

entry.title: I need to extend the cds add mta command
entry.content:
I work for the XSA team and need to extend the "cds add mta" command in order to make the output compatible  with the on-premise values.

Who can I contacts to help me with this task?

entry.title: SAP CAP : 502 "Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET"
entry.content:
Hi,

I am developing SAP CAP application, node.js, trying to connect to external S4 onPrem service which is accessed via the cloud connector.

I am trying to reach S4 OData service from CAP Application locally in Business Application Studio via SAP Cloud Connector.

To connect to the destination and other services we are using the “cds bind -2” command, which created the .cdsrc-private.json file.

I am able to connect to the different BTP instances like the destination, authorization and connectivity instances, when i run the app using 

cds watch --profile hybrid

But when i'm trying to access any of the entities within the S4 OData service facing the following error,

{"error":{"code":"502","message":"Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET"}}

Has anyone faced this issue, please suggest an appropriate solution for this.

Also when the applicaiton is deployed to BTP,  the S4 odata services are accessiable via destination and the application is working as expected,

i want run the application locally for development phase, hence need your help,

Regards,

Aakash

entry.title: CAP: "cds deploy --to hana --no-build" no longer works with @sap/cds-dk 9.0.6
entry.content:
Hello experts,

cds deploy --to hana --no-build

 is no longer working with  @SAP/cds-dk 9.0.6.

Error message:

One or more options are not supported by cds deploy to hana: --no-build

With @SAP/cds-dk  8.6.1 it is working.

Output of cds deploy --help for both versions:

SYNOPSIS

cds deploy [ <model> ] [ <options> ]

--no-build (only in combination with --to hana)

 EXAMPLES
cds deploy --to sqlite:db
cds deploy --to hana
cds deploy --to hana:myService --auto-undeploy
cds deploy --to hana --no-build

entry.title: How to bind to an existing service without knowing its name (Cloud Foundry / SAP BTP)?
entry.content:
I’m deploying an app to multiple subaccounts in SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry), and I need to bind to an existing aicore service instance.The issue is that each subaccount already has an aicore service, but with different names (e.g. my-aicore, aicore-prod, etc).

If I use this in mta.yaml:
It creates a new service, which I want to avoid.

  - name: generative-ai-hub
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service-plan: extended
      service: aicore

If I try this:
It gives an error because there’s no service named generative-ai-hub in the subaccount.

  - name: generative-ai-hub
    type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
    parameters:
      service-plan: extended
      service: aicore

Is there a way to bind to an existing service without knowing its exact name?
Or maybe some way to reference it dynamically?

entry.title: SFE annotations for dependent filtering on an Action Button's parameters do not work with array type
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

The title of my post basically explains the entire problem I'm having: I have a SAP Fiori Elements app that places an Action button that requires three parameters, among which there must be dependent filters. The specific annotation is as follows:

	actions
	{
		@(
			cds.odata.bindingparameter.name : '_it',
			Common.SideEffects :
			{
				TargetEntities : ['_it']
			},
			Core.OperationAvailable : _it.IsActiveEntity //avoids execution in Draft Mode
		)
		action addSItems(
			p_lob: String(30)
				@title : '{i18n>_LOBS}' 
				@Common.ValueList:
				{
					CollectionPath : 'LoBs',
					Label : '{i18n>_LOBS}',
					SearchSupported : true,
					Parameters : 
					[
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterOut',
							LocalDataProperty: p_lob,
							ValueListProperty: 'code',
						}
					],
				}
				@Common.ValueListWithFixedValues,

			p_barea: String(30)
				@title : '{i18n>_BUSAREA}'
				@Common.ValueList:
				{
					CollectionPath : 'BusinessAreas',
					Label : '{i18n>_BUSAREA}',
					SearchSupported : true,
					Parameters : 
					[
						// filtrar la bus. areas por la LoB seleccionada
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterFilterOnly',
							ValueListProperty: 'lob_code',
						},
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterIn',
							ValueListProperty: 'lob_code',
							LocalDataProperty: p_lob
						},
						///

						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterOut',
							LocalDataProperty: p_barea,
							ValueListProperty: 'code',
						},
					],
				}
				@Common.ValueListWithFixedValues,

			p_sitem: (30)
				@title: '{i18n>_SITEM}'
				@Common.ValueList:
				{
					CollectionPath : 'AUX_SItems',
					Label : '{i18n>_SITEM}',
					SearchSupported : true,
					Parameters : 
					[
						// filtrar por scope items no presentes aun en el mapa,
						// y por LoBs y Bus. Areas seleccionadas
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterFilterOnly',
							ValueListProperty: 'bMap_iid'
						},
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterFilterOnly',
							ValueListProperty: 'lob_code',
						},
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterFilterOnly',
							ValueListProperty: 'bArea_code',
						},
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterIn',
							LocalDataProperty: '_it/iid',
							ValueListProperty: 'bMap_iid'
						},
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterIn',
							LocalDataProperty: p_lob,
							ValueListProperty: 'lob_code'
						},
						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterIn',
							LocalDataProperty: p_barea ,
							ValueListProperty: 'bArea_code'
						},
						//////

						{
							$Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterOut',
							LocalDataProperty: p_sitem,
							ValueListProperty: 'code',
						}
					],
				}
		);
	};

Now, the problem is that when the data type of the parameters p_lob, p_barea, and p_sitem changes to  array of String (because I need a multi-valued select box), the dependent filtering no longer works. I have no clue why this happens.

Any help and/or comments that shed light on this are welcome.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: "cds add workzone" relativePaths: true is missing in ui5-deploy.yaml
entry.content:
Hi CAP team,

I have created a simple CAP project with a Fiori elements UI using the commands below:

cds init <projectname>
cds add tiny-sample
cds add xsuaa,hana --for production
cds add mta
<add UI>
cds add workzone
cds up

When I open the deployed app from HTML5 Applications menu, the app fails to fetch the metadata. The reason is that the uri of the service starts with a leading slash. In the Cloud Foundry environment, this should be removed.

    "dataSources": {
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/odata/v4/catalog/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [],
          "odataVersion": "4.0"
        }
      }
    }

If I create a standalone UI application, the `ui5-task-zipper` task in ui5-deploy.yaml is as follows:

    - name: ui5-task-zipper
      afterTask: generateVersionInfo
      configuration:
        archiveName: books-ui
        relativePaths: true
        additionalFiles:
          - xs-app.json

Whereas the same task with `cds add workzone` is missing the `relativePaths: true` :

    - name: ui5-task-zipper
      afterTask: generateVersionInfo
      configuration:
        archiveName: books-ui
        additionalFiles:
          - xs-app.json

With `relativePaths: true`, the leading slash is removed when the zip file is created. Therefore, I think this needs to be added when creating the ui5-deploy.yaml file by `cds add workzone`.

The output of `cds -v`

@cap-js/asyncapi: 1.0.3
@cap-js/openapi: 1.2.3
@sap/cds: 9.1.0
@sap/cds-compiler: 6.1.0
@sap/cds-dk (global): 9.1.0
@sap/cds-fiori: 2.0.1
@sap/cds-mtxs: 3.1.0
Node.js: v20.13.1
cap-ui-deploy-test: 1.0.0

entry.title: Multi-tenant CAP app with Fiori
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to explore the simplest multi-tenant CAP app using the samples. It has the bookshop backend and two frontend apps named browse and admin.

I have deployed the multi tenant app and can subscribe and view data accessing the backend API. But I am not able to access the Fiori Apps. It gives me the error saying 'File not found'.

The easiest way to replicate the same is using the template wizard for CAP.

Now, the app is deployed and subscribed from other subaccount. The service endpoint is accessible:

But the Fiori endpoint throws error:

What could be the issue here? Any help is highly appreciated.

entry.title: Unsupported media type 415 error when using cap-js/attachment via HTTP POST
entry.content:
I'm trying to upload an attachment via HTTP POST OData call to my CAP service entity that has an extended Attachments field/association from cap-js/attachments via a React external app, but I'm encountering a 415 HTTP error code. I can successfully create an entry to my main entity, but I'm unable to create an attachment due to the 415 error. Has anyone encountered this?

So far the terminal logs isn't showing any error, only in the browser.

entry.title: How to subscribe multi-tenant app in different global account.
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have a multitenant application hosted on SAP BTP which uses resources such as destination service and cloud connector. This works fine when our customers' subscribing subaccounts reside in the same global account that also keeps the subaccount that provides the application. I know this should be the preferred way to realize multitenant subscriptions. However, some customers would like to subscribe the app from within their own global account, i.e. a subaccount should subscribe to another subaccount in a different global account.

As described in this article service broker could be an option to do this but it comes with disadvantages (cloud connector/destinations cannot be used).

Is there another standard approach to subscribe to a multitenant app/subaccount in a different global account?

 

Many Thanks,

RK

entry.title: Local Mocking of External Service not working
entry.content:
I'm following the official documentation (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services) to integrate an external OData Service (API_BUSINESS_PARTER) while trying to mock it for local development. The steps I've taken:

  1. Download the edmx file from the Business Accelerator Hub
  2. import via "cds import <input_file> --as cds"
  3. Create srv/external/data/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER-A_BusinessPartner.csv and populate with example data from the docs
  4. Run the following code:
const bupa = await cds.connect.to('API_BUSINESS_PARTNER');
const { A_BusinessPartner } = bupa.entities;
const result = await bupa.run(SELECT(A_BusinessPartner).limit(100));

 In the logs I can see the following output for cds-ts watch

[cds] - mocking API_BUSINESS_PARTNER {
  impl: '../node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js',
  path: '/odata/v4/api-business-partner'
}

However, when the code above runs I'm getting the following error:

[error] - 500 > {
  code: '500',
  message: 'no such table: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER_A_BusinessPartner in:\n' +
    `SELECT json_insert(jsonb_insert('{}','$."BusinessPartner"',BusinessPartner,'$."Customer"'

 I would have expected the server to return the mock data from the csv file, but I can't get it to work. Anything I'm missing here that's not in the official documentation? I also ran "cds deploy --to sqlite" but it's not creating the table in question...

project dependencies:

"@sap/cds": "8.7.2",
"@sap/cds-dk": "8.9.4",
"@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity": "4.0.2",
"@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "4.0.2",
"@sap-cloud-sdk/resilience": "4.0.2",
"@cap-js/cds-typer": "0.35.0",
"@cap-js/cds-types": "0.10.0",
"@cap-js/sqlite": "1.11.1",
"@sap/cds-dk": "8.9.4",
"@sap/cds-lsp": "8.9.1",
